page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-6xCPU-24GB is a general-purpose virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It features 6 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz, with 6 physical cores and 1.0 thread per core. The system is equipped with 24.0 GB of RAM, yielding 4.0 GB of memory per core. No local storage or dedicated GPUs are bundled with this instance.

Benchmark data reveals a mixed performance profile. The shared CPU allocation results in poor single-core CPU performance with a stress-ng score of 781.78, placing it in the bottom 10%, and weak multi-core CPU performance with a stress-ng score of 4685.03, placing it in the bottom 25%. However, memory bandwidth remains average, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 622.27 GB/sec and uncached large block read speeds at 40.11 GB/sec. Passmark memory performance is weak at 1446.47, but database operations and Redis SET operations, which achieve 8.75 million operations per second, perform in the average tier.

Given the shared CPU architecture and lack of local storage, this server is positioned as a cost-efficient option for workloads that do not require sustained, high-performance compute. It is suitable for general-purpose applications, basic database operations, and lightweight text or image processing. The instance is less suited for high-throughput static web serving or intensive multi-threaded computing due to its lower-tier CPU benchmark results.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-6xCPU-24GB is an x86_64 virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor, featuring 6 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor at 2.0 GHz. It includes 24.0 GB of RAM but does not bundle local storage or GPU hardware. Performance benchmarks indicate a split profile: single-core and multi-core CPU performance are weak to poor, placing in the bottom 10% and 25% respectively, while memory bandwidth and Redis SET operations maintain average tier performance. This resource configuration offers a cost-efficient option for workloads with variable CPU demands. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, moderate database tasks, and lightweight text processing, whereas intensive multi-threaded computing and high-throughput web serving may be limited by the shared CPU allocation.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-6xCPU-24GB is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 6 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 24 GB RAM, offering average memory bandwidth and database performance for general-purpose workloads.

tagline: A shared-core virtual server with balanced memory capacity for cost-sensitive general-purpose applications and moderate database workloads.
